blockingIssues.description: `truck-bop/src/bounding.rs:39` in commit `ddb444cf9a57b7a024e91cf96d28b0e3a8eb48bb` bounds faces from boundary curves only, which can under-bound bounded surfaces with interior extrema; `truck-bop/src/bounding.rs:140` also lacks a regression that exercises actual surface coverage.

`truck-bop/src/broad_phase.rs:61` in commit `1405e0be` ignores `operand_rank` when generating BopDs-backed candidate pairs, so same-operand entities can be paired and sent to downstream intersection passes.

`truck-bop/src/intersect/vv.rs:10` in commit `55148ecf` requires an external vertex slice instead of using BopDs-registered geometry, leaving VV narrow phase disconnected from the BopDs-backed broad-phase workflow.
`truck-bop/src/intersect/ve.rs:23` in commit `73892e27` stores duplicate paves and VE interferences for repeated candidate pairs and lacks regression coverage for idempotent insertion.

`truck-bop/src/bounding.rs:202` in commit `3d2f74d3` removes the protruding-interior regression without replacing it with a valid non-planar regression, so the reported face-bounds failure mode remains unproven.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in `.factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md` that tests-first remains the default, but workers may introduce small helper scaffolding or replacement regressions in the same change when a strict red-first sequence is impractical or an existing red test is invalid, provided they document the deviation in handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reviews for `bounding-box-providers`, `ve-intersection`, `fix-face-bounding-box`, and `fix-broad-phase-bopds-integration` all cite justified deviations from the current strict wording in `.factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md`, and the handoffs already capture those deviations explicitly.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document in `.factory/library/architecture.md` that narrow-phase geometry must come from BopDs rather than side tables.
rejectedObservations.reason: recommend to orchestrator instead; the current architecture note is too high-level to update confidently without a broader design decision about BopDs ownership of geometry.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document in `.factory/library/architecture.md` that broad phase must filter same-operand pairs by `operand_rank`.
rejectedObservations.reason: recommend to orchestrator instead; this is a normative boolean-operation rule, not just a factual low-risk note.
